rulings.Case Title: RIMMA BOROKHOV et al VS. FREEDOM WEST HOMES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion To Enforce Subpoena
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 20, 2013, Line 6, PLAINTIFFS RIMMA BOROKHOV, DAVID TSE AND MICHAEL TO'S Amended Motion To Enforce Subpoena Pro Tem Judge Elizabeth Riles, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Court grants the request for judicial notice. The Court sustains the evidentiary objections to paragraphs 6, 8-12 and 14-17. The court overrules the evidentiary objections as to the other paragraphs. The court denies the motion without prejudice. The information involves private employment records of a third party. Therefore, the records must be directly relevant to the matter and the least intrusive means of discovery. The subpoena is not the least intrusive means to get the information sought. The Court urges the Plaintiff to take the deposition of Mr. Trotter to garner the necessary information, however, if after the deposition, the Plaintiff still feels that these records are necessary, the Plaintiff has the right to subpoena the records and seek an order from the Court for production, if necessary. Furthermore, the Court urges the parties to enter into an appropriate protective order should the documents need to be produced to protect the privacy of third party information. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to eriles@brhrlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: SUSAN V ANDREWS et al VS. TETRA TECH EC, INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, March 20, 2013, Line 32, DEFENDANT NEW WORLD ENVIRONMENTAL, INC's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained, with 10 days leave to amend, as to Defendant New World Environmental, Inc. dba New World Technology, Inc. ("Defendant New World") demurrer to the first and second causes of action on grounds of uncertainty. (CCP § 430.10(f).) As currently pled, the general allegations of each Plaintiff's employment relationship with the respective Defendants make the FAC uncertain. (See FAC 14 - 17.) Plaintiffs are given leave to amend to clarify which codefendant is being sued and facts to support a claim that New World retaliated against Jahr. The demurrers to the first and second causes of action for failure to state facts sufficient to state a cause of action (CCP §430.10(e)) are sustained with leave to amend on the same ground. Sustained, with leave to amend, as to the third cause of action for lack of jurisdiction (CCP § 430.10(a), and for uncertainty and failure to state facts sufficient to state a cause of action for the reasons set forth above. Sustained without leave to amend as to the fourth cause of action, based on Plaintiffs' concession that this cause of action is not pled against Defendant New World. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ALEJANDRO URRUZMENDI et al VS. THE CALIFORNIA INSTITUTE OF INTEGRAL STUDIES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Alejandro Urruzmendi, Erin Mcelroy, Jenna Haywood, Cory Era-Mcgarvey, On Behalf Of Themselves And Those Similarly Situated'S Motion To Compel Further Response To Plaintiffs' Request For Production Of Documents (Set 4) And Special Interrogatories (Set 2) And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 20, 2013, Line 7, Plaintiffs Alejandro Urruzmendi, Erin Mcelroy, Jenna Haywood, Cory Era-Mcgarvey, On Behalf Of Themselves And Those Similarly Situated'S Motion To Compel Further Response To Plaintiffs' Request For Production Of Documents (Set 4) And Special Interrogatories (Set 2) And For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Elizabeth Riles, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is granted in part and denied in part. Further responses as to RFPD Nos. 28, 29 and 30 and Special Interrogatory Nos. 8, 9 and 11 are ordered to be produced within 15 days of this order. The motion is denied as to the remaining discovery. RFPD Nos. 17-27 require production of private information. The third parties involved should have the opportunity to object to production of these documents. Special Interrogatory No. 10 is vague. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to eriles@brhrlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Sepcial Interrogatories And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 20, 2013, Line 4, DEFENDANT JOHN KITHAS Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Sepcial Interrogatories And For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Elizabeth Riles, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is granted in part and denied in part. Further responses as to Special Interrogatories Nos. 17 and 18 are ordered. These responses must comply with CCP 2030.230. If the response refers to documents, specific identification as to where the information can be found must be provided. Moreover, the original verifications for the Amended Responses must be provided and original verifications for the responses to the above ordered interrogatories must be provided. All to be produced within 15 days of this order. Further responses as to Special Interrogatory No. 21 is denied. The Interrogatory must be complete in and of itself. It cannot require referral to other documents to complete the response. No sanctions are awarded. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to eriles@brhrlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: DEBORAH JOHNS VS. CALIFORNIA CHECK CASH STORES LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, March 20, 2013, Line 23, DEFENDANTs COMMUNITY CHOICE FINANCIAL, INC., CALIFORNIA CHECK CASHING STORES, LLC & CHECKSMART FINANCIAL, LLC's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The unopposed motion to strike is granted with 20 days leave to amend to plead more facts to support the prayers for relief. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: DEBORAH JOHNS VS. CALIFORNIA CHECK CASH STORES LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, March 20, 2013, Line 23, DEFENDANTs COMMUNITY CHOICE FINANCIAL, INC., CALIFORNIA CHECK CASHING STORES, LLC & CHECKSMART FINANCIAL, LLC's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer is overruled. The objection raised to the complaint was based on CCP 430.10(f) [special demurrer for uncertainty]. Under the economic litigation rules, such demurrers are improper in limited jurisdiction cases. See CCP 92(c). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ZEYNEP AYNACI et al VS. TIMOTHY REMPEL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, March 20, 2013, Line 29, DEFENDANTs TIMOTHY REMPEL & REMPEL ARCHITECTURE, INC.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrer is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. See Price v. Dames & Moore (2001) 92 Cal.App.4th 355, 359-360. Plaintiff has leave to file and serve a certificate of merit and file an amended complaint pleading compliance with CCP 411.35. Plaintiff's complaint arises from professional negligence and requiring the certificate in the instant circumstances will further the purpose of CCP 411.35. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
